#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

case_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
config_file="${CONFIG:-$case_dir/buggy_config.json}"
runtime="${RUNTIME:-runc}"
rootfs_tar="${ROOTFS_TAR:-$case_dir/../../alpine-base.tar.gz}"
bundle="${BUNDLE:-$(mktemp -d)}"
container_id="${CONTAINER_ID:-youki-2756-$RANDOM}"

cleanup() {
  sudo -n "$runtime" delete -f "$container_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  sudo -n rm -rf "$bundle" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

mkdir -p "$bundle/rootfs"
if [[ ! -f "$rootfs_tar" ]]; then
  echo "missing rootfs tar: $rootfs_tar" >&2
  echo "set ROOTFS_TAR=/path/to/alpine-base.tar.gz or place alpine-base.tar.gz at the repository root" >&2
  exit 2
fi

sudo -n tar -xzf "$rootfs_tar" -C "$bundle/rootfs"
cp "$config_file" "$bundle/config.json"

sudo -n "$runtime" run -b "$bundle" "$container_id"

012|//  3. bpf program will check rule one by one in *reversed* order, return action of first rule
013|//     which matches device access operation

047|impl Devices {
048|    pub fn apply_devices(
049|        cgroup_root: &Path,
050|        linux_devices: &Option<Vec<LinuxDeviceCgroup>>,
051|    ) -> Result<(), DevicesControllerError> {
052|        tracing::debug!("Apply Devices cgroup config");
053|
054|        // FIXME: should we start as "deny all"?
055|        let mut emulator = emulator::Emulator::with_default_allow(false);
056|
057|        // FIXME: apply user-defined and default rules in which order?
058|        if let Some(devices) = linux_devices {
059|            for d in devices {
060|                tracing::debug!("apply user defined rule: {:?}", d);
061|                emulator.add_rule(d);
062|            }
063|        }
064|
065|        for d in [
066|            default_devices().iter().map(|d| d.into()).collect(),
067|            default_allow_devices(),
068|        ]
069|        .concat()
070|        {
071|            tracing::debug!("apply default rule: {:?}", d);
072|            emulator.add_rule(&d);
073|        }
074|
075|        let prog = program::Program::from_rules(&emulator.rules, emulator.default_allow)?;
076|
077|        // Increase `ulimit -l` limit to avoid BPF_PROG_LOAD error (#2167).
078|        // This limit is not inherited into the container.
079|        bpf_prog::bump_memlock_rlimit()?;
080|        let prog_fd = bpf_prog::load(LICENSE, prog.bytecodes())?;
081|
082|        // FIXME: simple way to attach BPF program
083|        //  1. get list of existing attached programs
084|        //  2. attach this program (not use BPF_F_REPLACE, see below)
085|        //  3. detach all programs of 1
086|        //
087|        // runc will use BPF_F_REPLACE to replace currently attached program if:
088|        //   1. BPF_F_REPLACE is supported by kernel
089|        //   2. there is exactly one attached program
090|        // https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/blob/8e6871a3b14bb74e0ef358aca3b9f8f9cb80f041/libcontainer/cgroups/ebpf/ebpf_linux.go#L165
091|        //
092|        // IMHO, this is too complicated, and in most cases, we just attach program once without
093|        // already attached programs.
094|
095|        // get the fd of the cgroup root
096|        let fd = nix::dir::Dir::open(
097|            cgroup_root.as_os_str(),
098|            OFlag::O_RDONLY | OFlag::O_DIRECTORY,
099|            Mode::from_bits(0o600).unwrap(),
100|        )?;
101|
102|        // collect the programs attached to this cgroup
103|        let old_progs = bpf_prog::query(fd.as_raw_fd())?;
104|        // attach our new program
105|        bpf_prog::attach(prog_fd, fd.as_raw_fd())?;
106|        // detach all previous programs
107|        for old_prog in old_progs {
108|            bpf_prog::detach2(old_prog.fd, fd.as_raw_fd())?;
109|        }
110|
111|        Ok(())
112|    }
